heading: Transparency and accountability for nonpostal services
label: 3705 Transparency and accountability for nonpostal services

children.heading: Annual Report to the Commission.—
children.label: a Annual Report to the Commission.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the last day of each year, the Postal Service shall submit to the Postal Regulatory Commission a report that analyzes costs, revenues, rates, and quality of service for each agreement or substantially similar set of agreements for the provision of property or nonpostal services under section 3703 or the program as a whole under section 3704, and any other nonpostal service authorized under this chapter, using such methodologies as the Commission may prescribe, and in sufficient detail to demonstrate compliance with the requirements of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Supporting matter
children.label: 2 .—Supporting matter
children.text: A report submitted under paragraph (1) shall include any nonpublic annex, the working papers, and any other supporting matter of the Postal Service and the Inspector General related to the information submitted in such report.

children.heading: Content and Form of Report.—
children.label: b Content and Form of Report.—
A
children.text: providing the public with timely, adequate information to assess compliance;

children.label: B
children.text: avoiding unnecessary or unwarranted administrative effort and expense on the part of the Postal Service; and

children.label: C
children.text: protecting the confidentiality of information that is commercially sensitive or is exempt from public disclosure under .section 552(b) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: the attribution of costs or revenues to property, products, or services under this chapter has become significantly inaccurate or can be significantly improved;

children.label: B
children.text: the quality of service data provided to the Commission for a report under this chapter has become significantly inaccurate or can be significantly improved; or

children.label: C
children.text: such revisions are, in the judgment of the Commission, otherwise necessitated by the public interest.

children.heading: .—Audits
children.label: c .—Audits
children.text: The Inspector General shall regularly audit the data collection systems and procedures used in collecting information and preparing the report required under subsection (a). The results of any such audit shall be submitted to the Postal Service and the Postal Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: If the Postal Service determines that any document or portion of a document, or other matter, which it provides to the Postal Regulatory Commission in a nonpublic annex under this section contains information described in section 410(c), or exempt from public disclosure under , the Postal Service shall, at the time of providing such matter to the Commission, notify the Commission of its determination, in writing, and describe with particularity the documents (or portions of documents) or other matter for which confidentiality is sought and the reasons therefor.section 552(b) of title 5

children.heading: .—Treatment
children.label: 2 .—Treatment
children.text: Any information or other matter described in paragraph (1) to which the Commission gains access under this section shall be subject to paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 504(g) in the same way as if the Commission had received notification with respect to such matter under section 504(g)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: provide an opportunity for comment on such report by any interested party; and

children.label: B
children.text: appoint an officer of the Commission to represent the interests of the general public.

children.heading: .—Determination of compliance or noncompliance
children.label: 2 .—Determination of compliance or noncompliance
children.text: Not later than 90 days after receiving a report required under subsection (a), the Postal Regulatory Commission shall make a written determination as to whether the activities carried out pursuant to this chapter during the applicable year were or were not in compliance with the provisions of this chapter. For purposes of this paragraph, any case in which the requirements for coverage of costs attributable have not been met shall be considered to be a case of noncompliance. If, with respect to a year, no instance of noncompliance is found to have occurred, the determination shall be to that effect. Such determination of noncompliance shall be included with the annual compliance determination required under section 3653.

children.heading: .—Noncompliance
children.label: 3 .—Noncompliance
children.text: If a timely written determination of noncompliance is made under paragraph (2), the Postal Regulatory Commission shall take appropriate action. If the requirements for coverage of costs attributable specified by this chapter are not met, the Commission shall, within 60 days after the determination, prescribe remedial action to restore compliance as soon as practicable, including the full restoration of revenue shortfalls during the following year. The Commission may order the Postal Service to discontinue a nonpostal service under section 3703 that persistently fails to meet cost coverage requirements.

children.heading: .—Deliberate noncompliance
children.label: 4 .—Deliberate noncompliance
children.text: In the case of deliberate noncompliance by the Postal Service with the requirements of this chapter, the Postal Regulatory Commission may order, based on the nature, circumstances, extent, and seriousness of the noncompliance, a fine (in the amount specified by the Commission in its order) for each incidence of such noncompliance. All receipts from fines imposed under this subsection shall be deposited in the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: .—Regulations Required
children.label: f .—Regulations Required
children.text: The Postal Regulatory Commission shall issue such regulations as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: .—Experimental Product Authority
children.label: g .—Experimental Product Authority
children.text: The Postal Service may conduct market tests of agreements pursuant to section 3703 and section 3704 of this chapter subject to the same terms and conditions described in . For the purposes of a market test, the cost coverage requirements in sections 3703 and 3704 of this chapter shall not apply.section 3641 of this title
